items.value: Temporarily replaces the specified material's texture with the composite result to check the appearance in the 3D view.
Specifies the target material for preview. Setting this will automatically enable the preview.
The texture property name of the material to be replaced with the composite result. You can select the texture item from the shader using the '▾' button on the right.
Select and specify the texture item from the material's shader.

Please specify a file in the 'PSD' field above and press 'Load'.
Reloading from history is also possible.

Displays the layer structure inside the PSD.
- Left eye toggle: Show/Hide
- Right dropdown: Blend Mode
- Expand folders or layers to display detailed parameters.
Displays a small comparison window of the Photoshop composite image (original look) included when the PSD file was created/saved in the bottom right of the preview.

Specifies the file format of the exported image.
- PNG: Exports the composite result as an alpha-enabled PNG.
- PSD: Exports as a PSD while maintaining current editing parameters.
- TGA: Exports in 32-bit (with alpha) TGA format.
* Opacity is not applied when using Pass-through
Adjusts the brightness of the layer (-150 to 150).
Adjusts the contrast of the layer (-50 to 100).
Adjusts the hue of the layer (-180 to 180 degrees).
Adjusts the saturation of the layer (-100 to 100).
Adjusts the lightness of the layer (-100 to 100).
Sets the fill color of the solid color layer.
Adjusts the opacity (alpha) of the layer between 0.0 (fully transparent) and 1.0 (fully opaque).
Enables applying hue and saturation to colorize B&W (achromatic) areas.
Inverts the RGB values of the layer's colors.
Enables converting the image into two tones: black and white.
Sets the threshold value (0 to 255) for dividing into two tones.
Enables posterization to reduce the number of colors for an illustration look.
Sets the number of levels (2 to 255) to represent.
Adjusts the input/output levels of highlights, shadows, and midtones to correct the brightness balance of the image.
Sets the input level (0-255) where the darkest part starts.
Sets the input level (0-255) where the brightest part ends.
Adjusts the brightness of the midtones (gamma value, 0.01-9.99). 1.0 is default.
Limits the minimum brightness (0-255) of the darkest part of the output image.
Limits the maximum brightness (0-255) of the brightest part of the output image.
Adjusts color balance (Cyan-Red / Magenta-Green / Yellow-Blue) for shadows, midtones, and highlights individually.
Maintains the brightness (luminosity) even when colors are changed.
Enables tone correction using a tone curve.
Finely adjusts input levels versus output levels using a graph editor.
Blends another external image fitted to the opacity shape (alpha) of this layer.
Specifies the texture image (Asset) used for blending.
Sets the horizontal and vertical tiling repetitions. 1.0 is actual size.
Sets the blend mode for blending the clipped image with the base image.
Enables applying a gradient color based on the luminosity (black and white brightness) of the image.
Automatically stretches the luminance range from the darkest to the brightest color in the layer to 0-1 so that the gradient map is applied evenly.
Edits the gradient used in the gradient map.
Gradient used in the gradient map
Sets the intensity (0.0 to 1.0) of applying the gradient map.
Generates a selection mask that extracts a specific color and its surrounding colors. You can select a color with the eyedropper and expand the range with the threshold.
Specifies the target color to extract.
Enables the eyedropper tool. You can pick the pixel color of the target layer directly by clicking on the preview.
Click on the target layer area in the preview to pick the color
Sets the tolerance range of color deviation from the specified target color. Larger values include a wider range of similar colors (0.0 is exact match only).
Ends the red selection highlight display on the screen.
Exports the current selection as white and everything else as black (including transparent parts) to a grayscale mask image (PNG) in the export folder.
This layer has no pixels, so the color range mask cannot be generated.
